text: A 40 μm sterile cell strainer (Corning, USA) is placed in a 6 cm cell culture dish containing 2 mL of cold PBS. Spleen tissue is positioned on the strainer and gently ground with the plunger of a 1 mL sterile syringe to obtain a single-cell suspension. The suspension is then centrifuged at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes at 4°C. After discarding the supernatant, 1 mL of ACK lysis buffer is added to lyse red blood cells at room temperature for 3 minutes. The reaction is stopped by adding 5 mL of cold PBS, followed by another centrifugation. The cells are then resuspended in 1 mL of PBS. Mouse femur and tibia are isolated and the bone marrow cavity is fully exposed. Bone marrow is flushed out with a sterile syringe containing 1 mL PBS, dispersed with a pipette, and filtered through a 40 μm cell strainer.
